title: [Spiritomb] Battle Guide

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[Spiritomb] earns its slot because of one single fact: [Ghost]/[Dark] is one of the only typings in the format with exactly one weakness. Base 108/108 defenses and [infiltrator] make it a dual-sided [wall] that also punishes [substitute] and screen-based teams. Base 50 HP keeps the total bulk lower than the numbers suggest, so the game plan is built around [infiltrator] mind-games and flexible use of [nasty-plot] rather than raw soaking.

## Strengths
The [Ghost]/[Dark] resistance profile is one of the best in the format — [Normal], [Psychic], and [Ghost] hits are blanked or halved, which means [Spiritomb] drops in on a huge range of pivots without paying for it. [infiltrator] is an underrated ability in this tier: it ignores [substitute] entirely, bypasses both [light-screen] and [reflect], and even punches through [safeguard], which removes several common stall defensive layers. Base 108/108 on both sides lets [Spiritomb] take one hit from almost any unboosted attacker, and a single [nasty-plot] turns its 92 SpA into a 184-effective attack number that completely changes role.

## Weaknesses & Answers
Base 50 HP is the actual bulk ceiling — 108/108 multiplied by 50 HP is only middling total bulk, and strong super-effective hits still bring [Spiritomb] into trade range. The single [Fairy] weakness is easy to exploit because the moment a [Fairy] appears [Spiritomb] has to rotate out, which costs the [wall] role its value. No real recovery means [leftovers] chip is the only way to stay healthy over long matches — stall or setup teams can out-last [Spiritomb] if nothing removes their win condition.

## Recommended Moveset
[will-o-wisp] anchors the [wall] role by halving the damage of every physical attacker that dares to trade with [Spiritomb]. [dark-pulse] is the STAB [Dark] special with a useful 20% flinch chance and benefits from [infiltrator] by going through screens. [shadow-ball] is the secondary STAB and the [Psychic]/[Ghost] coverage slot. The final slot is [nasty-plot], which enables the hidden offensive mode — after one boost, [Spiritomb] is a real special attacker, not just a [wall]. [sucker-punch] is a legitimate alternative if the team needs priority to patch the 35 Speed problem.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[leftovers] is the default item because [Spiritomb] has no recovery move — every long-game plan depends on the [leftovers] tick. [colbur-berry] is the specific answer for teams that carry priority [Dark] attackers, giving [Spiritomb] one buffered turn against [knock-off] or [sucker-punch]. [infiltrator] is the locked ability — pressure does nothing relevant. For Tera, [Fairy] completely removes the only weakness and is the textbook pick; Tera [Dark] is the alternative if you want to push [dark-pulse] and [shadow-ball] to their damage ceiling.

## Teammates & Synergy
[Spiritomb] needs a hard answer to [Fairy] types — [Steel] or [Poison] pivots work best. A setup sweeper behind it benefits from the [will-o-wisp] cushion [Spiritomb] creates on physical threats. Heal-bell or status-removal [support] lets [Spiritomb] stay in longer against opposing status spam, which it cannot self-cleanse.

## Play Patterns
Lead with [Spiritomb] on physical threats, burn the attacker with [will-o-wisp], and ride [leftovers] while the [rest] of the team sets up in the back. Save [nasty-plot] for the mid-to-late game, only after the opposing [Fairy] types are gone — the [wall] role evaporates the moment a [Fairy] is revealed. Use [dark-pulse] flinches as a soft tempo tool rather than relying on them.
